##### About me

Hello, I'm Cam—a passionate birth and postpartum doula on a mission to provide unwavering support to individuals and families throughout the incredible journey of childbirth and early parenthood. My own transformative experiences as a mother ignited a deep commitment to empower and nurture others during this pivotal chapter of life, leading me to embark on the fulfilling path of doula work.

My journey as a birth doula revolves around creating positive and empowering birthing experiences. I'm dedicated to offering emotional encouragement, evidence-based information, and physical comfort to expectant parents as they navigate the intricacies of labor and delivery. Advocating for birthing individuals, I ensure they feel informed, empowered, and supported in making choices aligned with their values and preferences. Seamlessly transitioning into my role as a postpartum doula, I extend support to new parents, providing guidance on newborn care, breastfeeding, and postpartum recovery. My non-judgmental and compassionate approach fosters a secure space, allowing families to thrive during the transformative early days of parenthood.

##### Service introduction

As a dedicated birth and postpartum doula, I offer a comprehensive range of services designed to support individuals and families through the transformative journey of childbirth and early parenthood. Throughout the birthing process, my role as a birth doula involves providing emotional encouragement, evidence-based information, and physical comfort to create a positive and empowering experience. I advocate for birthing individuals, ensuring they feel informed and supported in making choices aligned with their preferences. In the postpartum period, I seamlessly transition into offering guidance on newborn care, breastfeeding, and postpartum recovery. My non-judgmental and compassionate approach creates a nurturing environment, fostering the well-being of families as they navigate the joys and challenges of early parenthood. Whether it's lending a listening ear, offering informational support, or providing hands-on assistance, my goal is to tailor my services to meet the unique needs of each client, empowering them to embark on their parenting journey with confidence and peace of mind.

##### Packages

Pregnancy & Labor Support

Introducing the "Blossom Birth Experience" package—an all-encompassing support package tailored to enhance your birthing journey. This inclusive package offers two prenatal visits, delving into your birth preferences and addressing any concerns, ensuring you're well-prepared for the big day. On the day of delivery, I provide continuous emotional support, comfort measures, and advocacy. Following the birth, you'll receive two postpartum visits for debriefing, newborn care guidance, breastfeeding support, and emotional reassurance. This comprehensive package is designed to empower and support you throughout the transformative experience of childbirth and early parenthood, ensuring a positive and holistic birthing journey.

Nurturing postpartum bliss

Introducing the "Nurturing Postpartum Bliss" package—an all-encompassing postpartum doula care plan crafted to provide comprehensive support during the crucial early weeks of parenthood. With this package, you'll receive personalized assistance from a compassionate postpartum doula, including four in-home visits to address your unique needs. These visits encompass guidance on newborn care, breastfeeding support, emotional reassurance, and practical assistance with household tasks. Whether it's offering a listening ear or sharing evidence-based information, my goal is to create a nurturing environment that fosters your well-being as you navigate the joys and challenges of early parenthood. The "Nurturing Postpartum Bliss" package is designed to ensure you feel supported, empowered, and confident during this transformative chapter of your life.

##### Frequently asked questions

Do you support All Births

Yes! Supporting birth diversity is at the forefront of my doula career. I support births from csections to freebirths


How will you involve my partner in the birthing process?

Partner involvement is a key focus of my approach as a doula. I believe in creating a collaborative and supportive environment during the birthing process. In our consultations, we'll discuss and customize a plan that ensures your partner feels engaged and empowered. This may include providing them with tools for comfort measures, coaching on supportive techniques, and ensuring they have a clear understanding of their role in advocating for your preferences. I'm here to enhance the birthing experience for both of you, fostering a shared and positive journey into parenthood.

The cost of a doula in Tyler, TX can vary depending on the experience and services offered by the doula. On average, the cost of hiring a doula in Tyler, TX can range from $800 to $2000. It is important to research and interview multiple doulas to find one that fits your budget and meets your specific needs.

The cost of a postpartum doula in Tyler, TX can vary widely depending on the individual doula's experience, services offered, and the length of time needed. On average, postpartum doula services in Tyler, TX can range from $25 to $40 per hour. Some doulas also offer package deals for a set number of hours or days. It's best to contact individual doulas in the area to get specific pricing information.

It's important to note that doula fees can vary significantly within the Tyler area, with some highly experienced or specialized doulas charging higher rates. Additionally, some doulas offer package deals that may include prenatal visits, continuous labor support, and postpartum support, which can affect the overall cost.


When considering hiring a doula in Tyler, it's advisable to research and interview several doulas to find one who aligns with your preferences, needs, and budget. During consultations, you can discuss their specific services and pricing structure to ensure you have a clear understanding of what is included and the associated costs. This process will help you find a doula who meets your needs and fits within your budget.

I believe every mom deserves to feel informed, respected, and well cared for — physically, emotionally, and spiritually — throughout her journey. I offer hands-on comfort measures, evidence-based education, advocacy in hospital settings, and continuous presence so you feel supported and confident during birth.\\
With a background in medical and therapeutic massage, I bring advanced bodywork skills into labor support — helping with comfort, relaxation, positioning, and nervous system regulation. I’m trauma-informed, intuitive, and experienced working alongside healthcare professionals, allowing me to support you confidently whether your birth follows your hopes or takes an unexpected turn.\\
Faith is an important part of who I am, and I naturally integrate prayer, encouragement, and spiritual support into my care. This is woven into the way I serve moms and families, with gentleness, reverence, and trust in God’s presence throughout birth.\\

Hi, I’m Emily! I’m a mama of four and a birth doula serving families throughout East Texas. Motherhood and birth have always been something deeply important to me, and after having my own children, I knew I wanted to support other women through one of the most vulnerable and powerful seasons of their lives.\\
\\
I truly love what I do. My goal as a doula is to help families feel supported, informed, and cared for throughout pregnancy, labor, birth, and postpartum. I support all types of births and believe every family deserves to feel heard, respected, and empowered in their experience. \\
I strive to create a warm, judgment-free space where you can ask questions, feel safe, and know you don’t have to do this alone.](https://www.bornbir.com/emily-barton)

Hi, I’m Mallory the heart behind The Crunchy-ish Doula.\\
I’ve been married to my husband for nine years, and together we’re raising three beautiful children: a curious 7 year old, a spirited 4 year old, and our sweet 2 year old. Motherhood has been one of my greatest teachers, and every birth story I carry my own and the ones I’m honored to witness has shaped the way I serve families.\\
I welcomed my first child in a hospital setting. While I’m grateful for that experience, it also opened my eyes to how unprepared and disconnected many women can feel during birth. After that birth, and through the heartache of several miscarriages, I spent a lot of time praying asking the Lord to show me my purpose and how He wanted to use my story.\\
In His perfect timing, God answered a lifelong prayer.\\
After the home birth of my second son, everything became clear. I experienced firsthand the power of education, support, and trust in the body God designed. That birth changed me and in 2021, I became a doula.\\
My calling is to walk alongside families with open hands and a steady presence, honoring both faith and informed decision making. I believe God created our bodies with intention and wisdom, and I also believe there are moments when medical intervention is necessary and life saving. My role is never to push an agenda, but to help you prepare, discern, and advocate with confidence and peace no matter where or how you give birth.\\
Birth is sacred. Mothers are strong. And no family should feel alone in this season.\\
It would be an honor to support you.](https://www.bornbir.com/mallory-villafuerte)

I graduated from Tarleton State University with a degree in Child Development and Family Studies, focusing on Family Life Education, and I'm also a certified prenatal fitness instructor—so I get how important it is to prepare both your body and mind for the birth you're praying for!

I support home births, birth center births, and hospital births within about an hour of Godley, Texas, including Fort Worth, Burleson, Benbrook, Mansfield, Venus, Cleburne, Joshua, Crowley, Blum, and surrounding areas.

**My goal is to help you feel fully informed, emotionally supported, and spiritually encouraged as you prepare for your unmedicated birth!**

_**My service offerings include...**_

- Birth Doula Support
- Private Childbirth Education Sessions
- Private Birth Planning Sessions
- Pregnancy Text Support

When I’m not supporting mamas as a doula, you’ll find me baking organic goodies, diving into Bible studies, sipping raw milk lattes, and spending time outside with my husband and daughter.

**I’d love to hear all about you and the birth you're preparing and praying for!**

## Meet Morgan

RN-BSN, CAPPA CLD (Certified Labor Doula), CAPPA CPD (Certified Postpartum Doula), Evidence Based Birth Instructor, CPPNP (Certified Postpartum Nutrition Professional)

![morgan with her certification](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/baefe5_17d7f0f68bd4447c9fdcd1b5ccad8a53~mv2.jpeg/v1/crop/x_0,y_72,w_1536,h_1803/fill/w_469,h_577,al_c,q_80,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/f28acf7f-0284-4c80-97d3-90c298081e08.jpeg)

##### Owner & Certified Doula at Bumping Beyond

##### ​

Hello! I’m Morgan, the founder and heart behind Bumping Beyond. I’m a CAPPA-Certified Birth and Postpartum Doula, Evidence Based Birth® Instructor, and Certified Postpartum Nutrition Professional. I’m passionate about empowering families to feel confident, supported, and educated through every stage—from the bump and beyond

![Morgan and her family](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/ac547b_59baf49f101d44349e492889f383a889~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_445,h_577,al_c,q_80,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/481916741_29651047471161019_501044986108221086_n.jpg)

​​

## My Why

My journey into birth work began in high school, when I first noticed disparities in women’s health care. That early awareness grew stronger in nursing school, where I had the privilege of working with families in maternal and child units. I quickly fell in love with caring for mothers and babies during such a vulnerable and transformative time.

I also began to see how deeply mothers need extra care and connection. So often, once the baby arrives, the focus shifts entirely to the newborn—leaving mothers longing to be seen, supported, and nurtured themselves. That’s where I come in: to bridge that gap and help families provide meaningful, nurturing support for the mother.

After welcoming my first baby during “Snowmageddon” in 2021, I worked as a postpartum nurse, supporting laboring families and cesarean births in the OR. I noticed a recurring theme—families weren’t always receiving the support, education, and reassurance they truly needed.

That realization lit a fire in me. I dove deeper into women’s health, taking specialized courses beyond nursing to expand my knowledge in pregnancy, birth, and postpartum care. Now trained through Evidence Based Birth® and certified as a labor and birth doula, postpartum doula, and in postpartum nutrition, I’m honored to work FOR families, providing deeply personalized, evidence-based support.

[Menu](http://doulamorgan.com/about-morgan.html#) [DOULA MORGAN](http://doulamorgan.com/) - [Home](http://doulamorgan.com/) - [Services](http://doulamorgan.com/services.html) - [About Morgan](http://doulamorgan.com/about-morgan.html) - [Contact](http://doulamorgan.com/contact.html) ## ABOUT ME ## **Since 2013, Morgan has practiced birthwork as a doula, social worker and health advocate.** ![Picture](http://doulamorgan.com/uploads/3/5/3/8/35386284/published/img-2892.jpg?1571721487) **Why did I choose to suppor** **t families through this crazy, unpredictable journey called birth?** It began with a love of kiddos. I've worked many jobs with little ones, all the way up to young adults, and one thing I learned through that is when the parents are supported, the kids are better off. Some of my previous jobs included working as a sex educator, a care provider for babies through teenagers involved in the child welfare system, and a case manager for unhoused young parents. I really love learning. I am drawn to health, health care, bodies, people, emotions, and all the gooeyness that puts us together. I love to research. I love to teach and ensure people get accurate information they can understand. Social work was my way of combining all my interests into a more well rounded career, and doula work has become my paint brush for how I use those skills. The more I work in community, the more I am committed to change work. Leading a community based doula organization in Austin is a huge part of me. I believe that no one should have to give birth alone or under supported. **I believe families should have access to quality, gentle, respectful care in ANY setting they choose**. However, this isn't a reality... yet. The maternity care crises we have in Texas and in the United States has led to Black women being 3-4 times more likely to die from pregnancy complications than white women, and nearly all of those deaths are preventable. Maternal morbidity (near miss events) are also at a high. The reasons for this equity are multifaceted, but it all boils down to racism and white supremacy being embedded into the systems (every system- from healthcare to policies to policing and more), and people upholding those systems willingly or unwillingly. I work to address the systems of inequity from multiple levels. First, I am dedicated to improving birth outcomes for Black women by being involved in dismantling white supremacy in my life and addressing structural racism and inequity when I see it. I work downstream- direct care with families as a doula and educator, and I work upstream- through legislative advocacy, leading GALS, and working with the Maternal Health Equity Collaborative and the Texas Doula Association. Want to learn more about maternity care in the US? Click [here](https://www.usatoday.com/in-depth/news/investigations/deadly-deliveries/2018/07/26/maternal-mortality-rates-preeclampsia-postpartum-hemorrhage-safety/546889002/).​ * * * * * * ## **Ask Me about my values:** | | | | --- | --- | | | ## .Summertime .table ​.Clay | * * * | | | | | --- | --- | --- | | ![Picture](http://doulamorgan.com/uploads/3/5/3/8/35386284/img-5875_orig.jpg)

# Birth and Postpartum Support

Pregnancy often brings a mix of anticipation, questions, and moments of uncertainty. Many families arrive here looking not just for information, but for steadier guidance and a clearer way to prepare for what lies ahead.

​

Our time together centers on preparation that feels grounded and integrated...rooted in thoughtful education, practical tools, and a steady, familiar presence. This includes bringing partners into the process, walking through decisions as they arise, and creating space for honest questions, concerns that naturally arise, and the quieter worries that often go unspoken.

​

I offer variations of in person and virtual prenatal, birth, and postpartum packages designed to carry you through pregnancy, birth, and the early postpartum period.

![Monstera Leaves Closeup](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/11062b_54ef6fb8ec744a24a353d6758db5286d~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_1087,h_554,al_c,q_85,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/11062b_54ef6fb8ec744a24a353d6758db5286d~mv2.jpg)

![_edited_edited.jpg](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/ae10e8_49872b6bed574a9393f959c85606b0b6~mv2.jpg/v1/crop/x_0,y_212,w_1440,h_1441/fill/w_448,h_448,al_c,q_80,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/_edited_edited.jpg)

![](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/ae10e8_7d4924544eb74811ac20d92c8e4a49d4~mv2.jpg/v1/crop/x_0,y_59,w_353,h_250/fill/w_397,h_281,al_c,lg_1,q_80,enc_avif,quality_auto/ae10e8_7d4924544eb74811ac20d92c8e4a49d4~mv2.jpg)

## What’s Included

​

Custom packages are available and and may include:

​

- The Contraction Code labor guide

- Three virtual or in person prenatal visits

- 24/7 access to phone/text support for urgent matters

- On-call availability from 37-42 weeks for continuous labor support for virtual or in person

- One postpartum follow-up visit

- 20 hours of additional support used over five "flexible support visits" which are usable before or after birth


## Prenatal Preparation

There’s no single right way to arrive at birth feeling “ready”, this work meets you where you are and builds from there but, preparation is a central part of the process. Prenatal visits include substantial childbirth education, tools, and resources intended to support meaningful preparation beyond our time together. I provide structure, guidance, and information to help you understand your options and build practical skills, but the depth of our sessions depends on how much you engage with that material outside of visits.​ My role is to offer the framework and structure; how that preparation is integrated shapes how much we’re able to build on it during labor and birth. Together, we collaborate to build skills, understanding, and confidence over time.

​

The three prenatal visits include in-depth childbirth education focused on understanding labor physiology, anticipating common decision points, and developing practical coping strategies that support adaptability and informed participation throughout labor.

​

Topics commonly include:

​

- Labor physiology and what to expect as labor progresses

- Comfort measures and coping strategies

- Movement, positioning, and Spinning Babies–informed techniques

- Preparing the body for birth

- Natural induction methods

- Partner roles and practical support skills

- Decision-making and communication with care providers

- Pain management options

- Risks and benefits of common medical interventions

- Preparing for postpartum needs and recovery


## Birth Support​

On-call availability begins at 37 weeks and continues through 42 weeks. (I will still be available for you if your labor happens to fall outside of this window, this is just the general timeframe most people are delivering!)

​

During early labor, support is provided by phone and text. This allows for ongoing check-ins, guidance around rest, timing, position changes, comfort strategies, and decision-making at home or hospital, while preserving energy and helping families stay oriented as labor unfolds.  Once labor becomes active, I provide continuous, in-person support through active labor, birth, and the hour after delivery as you and your baby settle. Before I leave, I also support the early initiation of feeding, helping families work toward a first latch when possible.

Care during labor includes hands-on comfort measures such as positioning support, counterpressure, massage, and guidance with movement, as well as pacing, breathing, rest, hydration, and nourishment. I help partners participate effectively, track labor patterns, and support communication with care providers and decision-making as situations arise. At the family’s request, I’m also happy to take photos at any point using their phone or camera.

​

I also bring a well-stocked birth bag with comfort measures and coping tools that I use regularly in labor. Tools are offered thoughtfully and selectively, based on how labor is progressing and what feels supportive in the moment. Support is adjusted moment by moment in response to what’s happening

(In the rare event that I am unavailable, a trusted backup doula is arranged to ensure continuity of care.)

## Postpartum Follow-Up     ​The 1 hour postpartum follow-up visit offers space to review the birth experience, address immediate questions, and assess early recovery and adjustment. We check in on physical recovery, feeding and infant care logistics, emotional well-being, and day-to-day needs, and identify next steps or additional resources if helpful.​ The focus is on practical support, clarification, and helping families feel oriented as they settle into the early postpartum period.​

![](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/nsplsh_116099722cb94c8ca348551b88cf9bbd~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_246,h_283,al_c,q_80,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/nsplsh_116099722cb94c8ca348551b88cf9bbd~mv2.jpg)

## Flexible Support Visits    ​This package includes five flexible support visits, each lasting up to four hours for a total of 20 hours of extra support. These visits may be used before or after birth, depending on what will be most supportive for your family. Selections are made prior to signing to ensure clarity around planning and availability from the options below.

### Prenatal Home Prep Visit

Hands-on support focused on preparing the home and logistics for the postpartum period before baby arrives. This may include:

​

- Light cleaning or organizing related to postpartum needs

- Setting up recovery spaces and baby care areas

- Organizing supplies for feeding, recovery, and rest

- Preparing postpartum care items (such as padsicles)

- Light meal/snack prep or kitchen organization


This visit is intended to reduce physical and mental workload after birth by completing practical tasks in advance.

![](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/ae10e8_43767f6462fa4289b1bc68d13079d040~mv2.png/v1/crop/x_468,y_0,w_1739,h_1406/fill/w_208,h_168,al_c,q_85,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/ae10e8_43767f6462fa4289b1bc68d13079d040~mv2.png)

![JjMgyfkA-1100x1467_edited.jpg](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/ae10e8_8fe21afd8c7a4d81bdea5c70a3fb7230~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_208,h_233,al_c,q_80,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/JjMgyfkA-1100x1467_edited.jpg)

### Postpartum Support Visit

Hands-on support after birth, functioning as a postpartum doula shift. (Daytime support only; overnights are not offered.) This may include:

​

- Infant care support while parents rest, shower, or eat

- Postpartum education

- Practical household tasks related to immediate needs

- Light cooking/cleaning

- Emotional presence and check-in

- Light errands or grocery shopping


These visits focus on steadiness, relief, and practical support during the early postpartum period.

![Monstera Leaves Closeup](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/11062b_54ef6fb8ec744a24a353d6758db5286d~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_69,h_46,al_c,q_80,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,blur_2,enc_avif,quality_auto/11062b_54ef6fb8ec744a24a353d6758db5286d~mv2.jpg)

![iStock-1297036294_edited.jpg](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/ae10e8_b6b206dfd16446038cb283ee1312ea96~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_271,h_398,al_c,q_80,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/iStock-1297036294_edited.jpg)

## Pricing

Birth Preparation Only Package: $1000.00

Labor guide, 3 prenatal meetings, 24/7 phone availability for questions between meetings and before birth only, 1 postpartum follow up

​

Virtual Birth Package: $2,000.00

Labor guide, 3 virtual prenatal meetings, 24/7 phone access before, during, and after labor, 1 postpartum follow up

​

Full Birth Package: $3,500.00

Labor guide, 3 in person prenatal meetings, 24/7 phone access, in person labor support, 1 postpartum follow up

​

Birth and Support Visits/Postpartum Package: $5,000.00

Labor guide, 3 prenatal meetings, 24/7 phone access, in person labor support, 1 postpartum follow up, 20 hours of additional support

​

Support Visits/Postpartum Package: $1,500.00

1 postpartum prep/planning session, 20 hours of support

Insurance

​

​

Many insurance plans now offer doula benefits through reimbursement or approved doula programs. Coverage, reimbursement amounts, and documentation requirements vary by plan.

​

I am a Carrot approved doula and can provide detailed receipts or documentation for Carrot and other insurance plans as needed. Clients are responsible for confirming coverage and reimbursement eligibility directly with their insurance provider.

​

I do work with Kaiser Permanente clients; however, I am not contracted with Kaiser’s doula program. Kaiser clients are welcome to work with me on a private-pay basis and would be responsible for payment out of pocket.

![Monstera Leaves Closeup](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/11062b_54ef6fb8ec744a24a353d6758db5286d~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_69,h_46,al_c,q_80,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,blur_2,enc_avif,quality_auto/11062b_54ef6fb8ec744a24a353d6758db5286d~mv2.jpg)

![Understanding_Postpartum_Recovery_edited](https://static.wixstatic.com/media/ae10e8_746f3021eb714e1e85d18703c82f5520~mv2.jpg/v1/fill/w_271,h_398,al_c,q_80,usm_0.66_1.00_0.01,enc_avif,quality_auto/Understanding_Postpartum_Recovery_edited.jpg)
